name: Local 1233
description_short: Local 1233 Federal Credit Union is a membership-based financial institution serving ILA longshoremen and their families in the Newark, NJ area with savings accounts, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Local 1233 Federal Credit Union was founded nearly 50 years ago by Marvin Sykes, a member of ILA Local 1233 (International Longshoremen's Association), to serve the financial needs of port workers and their families in Newark, New Jersey. The credit union operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution, emphasizing community service and accessibility for its specific membership base.

The credit union offers core banking services including savings accounts, car loans, digital banking platforms, a mobile app (available on iOS and Android), payroll deposit services, and reloadable cards. Members can access savings products, various loan types with competitive rates, travel services, and insurance products through TruStage. The institution provides both in-person and digital channels for account management, with phone and email support available during business hours.

Local 1233 distinguishes itself through its tight-knit membership focus—eligibility is restricted to ILA Local 1233 and Local 2049 members, employees, and their immediate families. This focused membership model allows the credit union to tailor services specifically to longshore workers' financial needs. The institution maintains low account opening requirements ($30 for adults, $15 for minors) and offers payroll-integrated deposit systems. The credit union's governance includes a Board of Directors and transparent fee schedules published online.

The credit union operates with standard banking hours (Mon-Fri, 8:30am-4pm EST, Thursday until 5pm) and maintains straightforward policies around account access, withdrawals, and membership requirements. However, potential members should note the restrictive eligibility criteria and mandatory payroll deposit requirements, which limit accessibility compared to traditional banks. The institution's focus on a specific labor union membership creates both community strength and practical limitations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Digital banking platform with online account management
Mobile app for iOS and Android devices
Payroll deposit services with automatic contributions
TruStage life insurance without medical exams
Account withdrawal services with daily processing
Account enrollment and digital banking setup
Customer support via phone (973-824-7246) and email (info@local1233fcu.org)
pros: Low account opening minimum of $30 for adults and $15 for minors with joining fee structure
Modern digital banking infrastructure with native mobile apps for both iOS and Android
Payroll-integrated deposit system allows automatic regular contributions for members
Specialized car loan products designed for longshoremen and port workers
No medical exam requirements for TruStage life insurance offerings
Clear, published fee schedules and account policies available online
Long operational history (nearly 50 years) serving the same membership community
cons: Strict membership eligibility limited to ILA Local 1233, Local 2049 members, and their immediate families only—not open to general public
Mandatory payroll deposit requirement for all members; changes must go through the credit union rather than directly with employer
Withdrawal limits enforced with $25 per withdrawal fee after 15 withdrawals annually, which could burden members with frequent cash needs
Minimum $50 account balance required at all times; accounts inactive for 3+ years are automatically escheated to New Jersey
Limited geographic footprint with single Newark, NJ location; no indication of branch expansion or nationwide access
best_for: ILA longshoremen and port workers seeking credit union services tailored to their industry
Families of union members who are eligible through kinship relationships
Members prioritizing digital banking convenience with mobile app access
Borrowers seeking car loans from a credit union familiar with maritime workers' financial profiles
diagnosis: Local 1233 Federal Credit Union is best for active and retired ILA longshoremen and their immediate family members who want credit union banking services integrated with payroll deposits. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to this specific union community, making it inaccessible to the general public, and all members must establish mandatory payroll deposits with the institution.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
